entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop whose residues are used as fodder for ruminants, showing significant losses in foliage and yield due to diseases

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop whose residues are used as fodder, with residues (stover) traded in commercial markets

entities.name: PLANT DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions that reduce the quantity and quality of crop residues used as fodder

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: The remains of crops after harvest, serving as a major source of fodder and feed security for ruminants

entities.name: RUMINANTS
entities.description: Livestock animals that consume crop residues as fodder and are affected by the quality of the feed

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY RURAL APPRAISAL
entities.description: A socio-economic research method used to assess farmers' perceptions and the impact of plant diseases

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: The Indian state where the case studies for the socio-economic study were conducted

entities.name: DECCAN PLATEAU
entities.description: The geographic region in India where the study on crop residues and dairy production took place

entities.name: FEED SECURITY
entities.description: The reliable availability of fodder for ruminants, especially during the summer in dryland areas

entities.name: SUMMER
entities.description: The season during which crop residues are the primary source of feed security for ruminants

entities.name: DRYLAND AREAS
entities.description: Regions with limited water and fodder alternatives where farmers rely heavily on crop residues

entities.name: FOLIAR DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases affecting the leaves of crops, contributing to the overall impact of plant diseases

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that, along with diseases, cumulatively affect the quality and yield of crop residues

entities.name: QUALITY OF CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: The nutritive value and acceptability of fodder, which influences ruminant health and feed refusal

entities.name: FODDER YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of crop residues produced, which is reduced by plant diseases

entities.name: GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: A method to enhance the feed-quality of crop residues without compromising yield traits

entities.name: PEST AND DISEASE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Inexpensive and easy-to-use options to improve the quantity and quality of crop residues

entities.name: SORGHUM STOVER
entities.description: The specific crop residue of sorghum that is traded in commercial markets

relationships.description: reduces quality and quantity of residues
reduces quality and quantity of residues
